# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with additional EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: app
    Description: Prefix for resource names
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC for the instances
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet for the instances
  AmiId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Image::Id
    Description: AMI ID for the instances
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Existing EC2 KeyPair for SSH access
  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 2
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 2
    Description: Number of EC2 instances to launch
  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Size in GiB for each application data volume
  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 20
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Size in GiB for the root volume
  AllowedSshCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR allowed for SSH access

Conditions:
  CreateSecondInstance: !Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 2]

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedSshCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-instance-sg'

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ec2-role'

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: /
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  AppInstance1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref AmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-1'

  AppDataVolume1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt AppInstance1.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSize
      VolumeType: gp3
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-data-1'

  AppDataVolumeAttachment1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Ref AppInstance1
      VolumeId: !Ref AppDataVolume1
      Device: /dev/sdf

  AppInstance2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref AmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-2'

  AppDataVolume2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt AppInstance2.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSize
      VolumeType: gp3
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-data-2'

  AppDataVolumeAttachment2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Ref AppInstance2
      VolumeId: !Ref AppDataVolume2
      Device: /dev/sdf

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID for the application instances
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
  Instance1Id:
    Description: EC2 instance ID for app instance 1
    Value: !Ref AppInstance1
  Instance1PrivateIp:
    Description: Private IP of app instance 1
    Value: !GetAtt AppInstance1.PrivateIp
  Volume1Id:
    Description: EBS volume ID attached to app instance 1
    Value: !Ref AppDataVolume1
  Instance2Id:
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Description: EC2 instance ID for app instance 2
    Value: !Ref AppInstance2
  Instance2PrivateIp:
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Description: Private IP of app instance 2
    Value: !GetAtt AppInstance2.PrivateIp
  Volume2Id:
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Description: EBS volume ID attached to app instance 2
    Value: !Ref AppDataVolume2
